description_long: This bollard is a tall, slender post with a square base and a slightly wider, ornate cap at the top. The surface appears textured and weathered, with a greenish hue suggesting moss, patina, or painted metal, giving it an aged, robust look. The shaft is straight and uniform, with subtle decorative elements just below the cap and above the base, adding a touch of classical architectural style. The base is solid and broad, providing stability, while the cap features a beveled, blocky design with additional ornamental details. There are no visible openings, chains, or connectors, and the object appears to be a single, solid piece. Its overall appearance suggests it is designed to serve as a fixed barrier in urban environments, such as sidewalks, parks, or building entrances.
description: A tall, slender, greenish bollard with a square, ornate cap and a solid, broad base, featuring classical decorative details and a weathered texture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height, ornate cap, and broad base with visible decorative elements.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, highlighting the straight shaft and symmetrical cap and base.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, with the same decorative cap and base details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the uniform shaft and the ornate cap and base.
